title: dactylorchis majalis var. laxa
intro: Dactylorchis majalis var. laxa, also known as the Dactylorhiza laxa, is a rare and endangered species of orchid, characterized by its distinctive greenish-yellow flowers and elongated spikes. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as the ability to stimulate the immune system. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza laxa include the potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza laxa typically involve drying the plant's roots and leaves, which can then be used to create teas, tinctures, or infusions.
